sentences.text: UnitedHealthcare Individual Exchange This Medical Policy applies to Individual Exchange benefit plans.
sentences.label: NON_ARGUMENTATIVE

sentences.text: Pulsed Radiofrequency Ablation: Technique that delivers intermittent, short bursts of energy, instead of continuous energy, using a probe temperature of 42 to 45 °C (Hayes, 2023).
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: Applicable Codes The following list(s) of procedure and/or diagnosis codes is provided for reference purposes only and may not be all inclusive.
sentences.label: NON_ARGUMENTATIVE

sentences.text: Listing of a code in this policy does not imply that the service described by the code is a covered or non-covered health service.
sentences.label: NON_ARGUMENTATIVE

sentences.text: Benefit coverage for health services is determined by the member specific benefit plan document and applicable laws that may require coverage for a specific service.
sentences.label: CLAIM

sentences.text: The inclusion of a code does not imply any right to reimbursement or guarantee claim payment.
sentences.label: NON_ARGUMENTATIVE

sentences.text: CPT Code Description 22899 Unlisted procedure, spine [when used to report the Intracept procedure or cooled radiofrequency ablation] 27299 Unlisted procedure, pelvis or hip joint 64625 Radiofrequency ablation, nerves innervating the sacroiliac joint, with image guidance (i.e., fluoroscopy or computed tomography) 64628 Thermal destruction of intraosseous basivertebral nerve, including all imaging guidance; first 2 vertebral bodies, lumbar or sacral 64629 Thermal destruction of intraosseous basivertebral nerve, including all imaging guidance; each additional vertebral body, lumbar or sacral (List separately in addition to code for primary procedure) 64999 Unlisted procedure, nervous system CPT® is a registered trademark of the American Medical Association Description of Services Pulsed Radiofrequency Ablation delivers short bursts of radiofrequency energy instead of the conventional technique of continuous energy, allowing the tissue to cool between bursts in a pulsed manner (Hayes, 2023).
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: Endoscopic rhizotomy, a posterior endoscopic method, also known as dorsal endoscopic rhizotomy, has been developed as an alternative to percutaneous electrode radiofrequency ablation to target the medial, intermediate, and lateral branches of the dorsal ramus using a modification of the Yeung Endoscopic Spinal Surgery cannula and a specially designed Ellman radiofrequency bipolar electrode.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: Cooled radiofrequency (e.g., COOLIEF) transmits thermal radiofrequency energy using water-cooled electrodes/probes.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: Chemical ablation uses an injection of chemicals, such as phenol or alcohol, to destroy nerve tissue.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: Laser ablation destroys nerve tissue using a laser beam.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: Ablative Treatment for Spinal Pain Page 2 of 14 UnitedHealthcare Commercial and Individual Exchange Medical Policy Effective 02/01/2026 Proprietary Information of UnitedHealthcare.
sentences.label: NON_ARGUMENTATIVE

sentences.text: Copyright 2026 United HealthCare Services, Inc. Clinical Evidence Pulsed Radiofrequency Ablation There is insufficient evidence to establish the safety and efficacy of pulsed radiofrequency ablation (RFA) for treating spinal pain.
sentences.label: CLAIM

sentences.text: Well-designed randomized controlled trials (RCTs), with large sample sizes and long-term follow-up, are needed to establish the impact on health outcomes.
sentences.label: CLAIM

sentences.text: An Agency for Healthcare Research and Quality comparative effectiveness review evaluated pulsed RFA for treating facet joint pain in the Medicare population.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: The report concluded that the evidence is insufficient to assess pulsed RFA for presumed facet joint pain vs sham denervation or continuous radiofrequency (CRF) denervation (Chou et al., 2021).
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: In 2021, Hayes updated a 2019 Health Technology Assessment for the use of pulsed radiofrequency (PRF) application to the dorsal root ganglion (DRG) for the treatment of cervical radicular pain that has failed to respond to conservative treatment.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: The report concluded that a very low-quality, limited body of evidence suggests that PRF application to the DRG may reduce pain in individuals with cervical radicular pain that has failed to respond to conservative treatment; however, the body of evidence is insufficient to draw definitive conclusions.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: Considerable limitations to the body of evidence include a small evidence base, consisting of one fair- and three poor-quality RCTs; inconsistency of PRF treatment methods among studies; and lack of long-term follow-up.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: Additional robust, comparative evidence is needed to determine whether PRF application to the DRG is an effective and safe alternative treatment for cervical radicular pain that has failed conservative treatment. Kroll et al. (
sentences.label: CLAIM

sentences.text: 2008) compared the efficacy of CRF thermocoagulation with pulsed RFA in a prospective, randomized, double-blinded study in 50 participants with lumbar back pain.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: CRF thermocoagulation was delivered at 80 °C for 75 seconds, while PRF was delivered at 42 °C, with a pulse duration of 20 milliseconds and pulse rate of 2 Hz for 120 seconds.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: No significant differences in the relative percentage improvement were noted between groups in either visual analog scale (VAS) or Oswestry Low Back Pain and Disability Questionnaire (OSW) scores.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: In the PRF group, comparisons of the relative change over time in both VAS and OSW scores were not significant.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: The investigators concluded that although no significant difference between CRF and PRF therapy in long-term outcome in the treatment of lumbar facet syndrome was observed, a greater improvement over time was noted in the CRF group.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: Furthermore, the sample size may have been too small to detect clinically significant differences between the interventions. Chao et al. (
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: 2008) retrospectively reviewed a case series of 154 patients with lumbar or cervical radicular pain due to a herniated intervertebral disk or previous failed surgery to analyze the efficacy of percutaneous pulsed RFA.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: Patients had pulsed RFA in two to four spinal levels unilaterally, with follow-up from 1 week to 1 year post operation.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: Overall, 53% of 49 patients with cervical pain and 50% of patients with lumbar pain had an initial improvement of 50% or more in the first week of follow-up.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: In total, 55% of patients with cervical pain and 44% of patients with lumbar pain had pain relief of 50% or more at the 3-month follow-up.
sentences.label: NON_ARGUMENTATIVE

sentences.text: The authors concluded that pulsed RFA appears to provide intermediate-term relief of pain; however, further studies, with long-term follow-up, are necessary.
sentences.label: CLAIM

sentences.text: Limitations of this study include the lack of a comparison group, retrospective design, and inability to generalize results due to a wide range of follow-up.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: The patients were divided into three groups according to the etiology of the lesion herniated disk, spinal stenosis, and failed back surgery syndrome.
sentences.label: NON_ARGUMENTATIVE

sentences.text: The efficacy of the technique was assessed using a 10-point numeric rating scale (NRS) at baseline and the global perceived effect at 30, 60, 90, and 180 days.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: The reduction in medications and number of complications associated with the technique were assessed, although these were not reported.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: Pain reduction was noted in all groups, except for those with failed back surgery syndrome. No complications were noted.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: The authors concluded that PRF was effective in herniated disk and spinal stenosis but not failed back surgery syndrome.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: The flaws of this study include the lack of a comparison group undergoing a different treatment, retrospective design, subjective outcome measures, and short-term follow-up.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: Van Zundert (2007) studied the effect of pulsed RFA in participants with cervical radicular pain.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: Overall, 23 of 256 screened participants in a randomized sham-controlled trial met the inclusion criteria and were randomly assigned in a double-blinded fashion to receive either pulsed RFA for 120 seconds or sham intervention.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: At 3 months, the pulsed RFA group showed a significantly better outcome regarding the global perceived effect (> 50% improvement) and VAS (20-point pain reduction).
sentences.label: NON_ARGUMENTATIVE

sentences.text: The quality-of-life scales also showed a positive trend in favor of the pulsed RFA group, but significance was only reached in the 36-Item Short Form Survey (SF-36) Ablative Treatment for Spinal Pain Page 3 of 14 UnitedHealthcare Commercial and Individual Exchange Medical Policy Effective 02/01/2026 Proprietary Information of UnitedHealthcare.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: The need for pain medication was significantly reduced in the pulsed RFA group after 6 months.
sentences.label: NON_ARGUMENTATIVE

sentences.text: The authors concluded that these study results are in agreement with the findings of a previously completed clinical audit that suggested that pulsed RFA of the cervical DRG may provide pain relief for a limited number of carefully selected individuals with chronic cervical radicular pain, as assessed by clinical and neurological examination.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: Although the study results are promising for certain individuals, the small sample size, use of subjective outcomes, and lack of long-term follow-up minimize the generalizations of the conclusions.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: Endoscopic Radiofrequency Ablation/Endoscopic Rhizotomy There is insufficient evidence to establish the safety and efficacy of endoscopic RFA for treating spinal pain.
sentences.label: CLAIM

sentences.text: Well- designed RCTs, with large sample sizes and long-term follow-up, are needed to establish the impact on health outcomes. Du et al. (
sentences.label: CLAIM

sentences.text: 2024) conducted a systematic review and meta-analysis of 11 RCTs that compared the efficiency of percutaneous RFA and conservative treatment (sham procedures, facet joint injection, physiotherapy, exercise, or oral medication) or compared the efficiency of percutaneous RFA and endoscopic neurotomy for lumbar facet joint syndrome.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: Of these 11 articles, nine reported on the effects of percutaneous RFA, and two studies evaluated the efficiency of endoscopic neurotomy.
sentences.label: CLAIM

sentences.text: Of the two reporting on endoscopic neurotomy, the results showed that at 1 month, no difference between that and percutaneous RFA or placebo was observed.
sentences.label: CLAIM

sentences.text: Twelve-month results showed that when compared with RFA, endoscopic neurotomy can significantly reduce pain.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: The authors concluded that endoscopic neurotomy can be used successfully as a complement to the percutaneous technique in individuals with therapy-refractory low back pain (LBP).
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: Compared with percutaneous RFA, endoscopic neurotomy seems to reduce LBP for a longer period of time.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: Further research, with a longer follow-up period, is needed to confirm these findings.
sentences.label: CLAIM

sentences.text: Hayes published an Evidence Analysis Research Brief in 2024 to summarize and evaluate the evidence related to endoscopic rhizotomy for the treatment of LBP.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: A review of abstracts suggested that there is adequate published, peer- reviewed literature; however, conclusions about safety and effectiveness cannot be made in this report.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: 2020) conducted a prospective cohort study to assess the effectiveness of endoscopic rhizotomy for denervation of lumbar facet joints in participants with chronic low back pain (CLBP) due to facet joint syndrome.
sentences.label: NON_ARGUMENTATIVE

sentences.text: The study included 40 of 50 screened participants divided into two equal groups: group A participants were previously treated with percutaneous RFA (n = 20), and group B participants were having their first interventional treatment (n = 20).
sentences.label: NON_ARGUMENTATIVE

sentences.text: NRS and Oswestry Disability Index (ODI) scores were assessed before and after the procedure.
sentences.label: NON_ARGUMENTATIVE

sentences.text: NRS was reduced significantly after 1 month and remained the same until the end of the study.
sentences.label: NON_ARGUMENTATIVE

sentences.text: The ODI was significantly improved from 1 month after surgery up to the end of the study.
sentences.label: NON_ARGUMENTATIVE

sentences.text: Participants aged less than 60 years or who had one to two joints treated had better improvement than the others.
sentences.label: NON_ARGUMENTATIVE

sentences.text: The authors concluded that participants treated with endoscopic rhizotomy achieved pain relief through follow-up at 2 years.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: Study limitations include the lack of randomization and a control and the small sample size.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: Cryoablation There is insufficient evidence to establish the safety and efficacy of cryoablation for treating spinal pain.
sentences.label: CLAIM

sentences.text: Well-designed RCTs, with large sample sizes and long-term follow-up, are needed to establish the impact on health outcomes.
sentences.label: CLAIM

sentences.text: Overall, 210 participants with clinically suspected SIJ pain who obtained short-term benefit from diagnostic SIJ injections and prognostic lateral branch blocks were randomly assigned to receive CRFA of the L5 dorsal ramus and S1 to S3 lateral branches or SMM that included pharmacotherapy, injections into the sacroiliac ligaments or joint cavity, physical therapy, chiropractic care, lifestyle changes, acupuncture, and yoga.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: The primary outcome measure was mean reduction in LBP score via the numeric pain rating scale at 3 months.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: The secondary outcomes included quality of life and function, as assessed by the ODI, SF-36 physical function domain, and EuroQoL-5.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: Participant satisfaction was assessed using the Patient Global Impression of Change scale.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: The results showed that CRFA resulted in superior improvements across all domains compared with medical management.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: A total of 105 adverse events (CRFA: 65 events in 47 of 96 participants, 49.0%; SMM: 40 events in 28 of 104 participants, 26.9%) occurred.
sentences.label: CLAIM

sentences.text: In the CRFA cohort, 16 of these events were related to the procedure, while in the SMM participants, five events were related to procedures.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: The majority of CRFA-related adverse events involved worsening pain in the lower back or around the SIJ and reported postprocedural pain.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: No serious related adverse events were reported, including nerve injury, in either group.
sentences.label: NON_ARGUMENTATIVE

sentences.text: The authors concluded that CRFA is Ablative Treatment for Spinal Pain Page 4 of 14 UnitedHealthcare Commercial and Individual Exchange Medical Policy Effective 02/01/2026 Proprietary Information of UnitedHealthcare.
sentences.label: NON_ARGUMENTATIVE

sentences.text: Copyright 2026 United HealthCare Services, Inc. superior to medical management for treating chronic SIJ pain.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: This study is limited by a short follow-up period as well as the lack of specific requirements for the control group, which could undermine generalizability.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: Selection of participants was based on medical history, physical examination, and positive medial branch blocks.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: Percutaneous medial branch cryodenervation was performed using a Lloyd Neurostat 2000.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: However, the authors noted that at the 12-month follow-up period, the failure rate rose to 43%.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: The findings are limited by the lack of a comparison group.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: All participants received one treatment after confirmation with a medial branch block using a 1.3-cm needle.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: Twenty-six participants required two to four additional treatments, and a 2.0-cm needle was used.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: The VAS was used as an evaluation tool, along with reports of return to work and pain medication use.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: The authors concluded that the computed tomography–guided treatment was effective.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: The intervening variable of the medial branch blocks must be considered as part of the pain relief response, which the authors acknowledged.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: Overall, 50% of participants had 50% pain relief for at least up to 1 year in the reported aggregate data.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: Although the results are promising, further study is needed to identify the placebo effect of the medial branch blocks.
sentences.label: CLAIM

sentences.text: Cooled Radiofrequency Ablation for Facet or Sacroiliac Joints There is insufficient evidence to establish the safety and efficacy of CRFA for treating facet joint or sacroiliac pain.
sentences.label: CLAIM

sentences.text: Well- designed RCTs, with large sample sizes and long-term follow-up, are needed to establish the impact on health outcomes.
sentences.label: CLAIM

sentences.text: In 2022 (updated 2024), a Hayes Health Technology Assessment evaluated the safety and effectiveness of CRFA for the treatment of CLBP that originates from the SIJ.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: The report showed an overall low-quality body of evidence, which suggests that CRFA of the SIJ is safe and may be effective for reducing the intensity of CLBP and improving physical function for 6 to 12 months.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: Additional well-designed and well-executed research studies, with adequate sample sizes, are necessary to evaluate the treatment efficacy of CRFA.
sentences.label: CLAIM

sentences.text: An Agency for Healthcare Research and Quality comparative effectiveness review by Chou et al. (
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: CRFA for sacroiliac pain was associated with a moderate to large reduction in pain and small to large improvement in function vs sham radiofrequency at 1 month.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: Improvements in pain and function at 3 months were moderate. Evidence beyond 6 months is lacking.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: CRFA for presumed facet joint pain was associated with a small, non–statistically significant reduction in pain vs conventional RFA at 6 months and no difference in function.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: The authors concluded that when using a single diagnostic block paradigm with a threshold of > 75% pain reduction, treatment with both CRFA and traditional RFA resulted in a success rate of approximately 50% when defined by both improvement in pain and physical function at the 6-month follow-up.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: While the success rate was higher in the CRFA group, this difference was not statistically significant.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: Due to the small sample size, the lack of statistically significant findings could be due to type 2 errors, and the study should therefore be considered inconclusive.
sentences.label: CLAIM

sentences.text: 2018) conducted a meta-analysis to assess the efficacy and safety of using CRFA in treating individuals with chronic SIJ pain in terms of pain and disability relief, individuals' satisfaction degree, and complications.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: The authors suggested that high-quality and large-scale RCTs are required to validate their findings.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: The authors reported that the study results suggest that SInergy safely afforded patients greater and more durable analgesia and disability relief than Simplicity III for SIJ-derived pain.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: The Simplicity III procedure may be more conducive than SInergy for bilateral procedures and for individuals who have limited tolerance to be in an RFA procedure-required prone position.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: The authors concluded that the results show promising, durable improvements in pain, quality of life, and medication usage, with benefits persisting in some patients at 20 months after treatment.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: The study included patients aged 18 to 70 years, irrespective of sex, while specific inclusion and exclusion Ablative Treatment for Spinal Pain Page 6 of 14 UnitedHealthcare Commercial and Individual Exchange Medical Policy Effective 02/01/2026 Proprietary Information of UnitedHealthcare.
sentences.label: CLAIM

sentences.text: The authors concluded that despite potential side effects, such as transient pain, increased blood sugar levels, nerve damage, or infection, both treatments provided pain relief and reduced disability, presumably underscoring the importance of personalized treatment plans.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: This study has many limitations, including the retrospective design of the study, small sample size, exclusion of outliers, absence of a placebo or sham control group, methodological flaws, reliance on subjective data (ODI scores), limited follow-up duration, and low statistical power.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: The authors concluded that in this 5-year aggregate analysis, BVNA improved pain and function scores compared with baseline.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: Similarly, there were reductions in opioid consumption and spinal injections post BVNA.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: The authors concluded, in this aggregate analysis of 247 participants with vertebrogenic pain, that use of conservative care and opioids and the need for functional strengthening activities were substantially reduced through 5 years post BVNA compared with baseline.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: Lumbar fusion rates were less than half the published value at 5 years in similar populations.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.label: CLAIM

sentences.text: The authors concluded that BVNA, biological therapy, and multifidus stimulation provide improvement in both pain and disability compared with other interventions.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: The limited number of studies available has resulted in CIs that are too broad to declare a statistical difference and an inability to estimate accurate effects for each tested treatment.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: The authors concluded that BVNA, among other criteria, is a safe and minimally invasive therapy that significantly lowers pain and impairment in individuals with vertebrogenic pain with distinct Modic type 1 and 2 changes at L3-S1 vertebral levels.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: The authors concluded that BVNA appears to be a durable, minimally invasive treatment for the relief of CLBP that can be successfully implemented in a community practice setting.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: The authors concluded that intraosseous BVNA demonstrated clinically meaningful and durable improvements in pain and function through 3 years in participants with primary vertebrogenic LBP.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: BVNA-treated participants reduced opioid use and interventions for LBP.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: The authors concluded that there is moderate-quality evidence suggesting that this procedure is effective in reducing pain and disability in individuals with CLBP with type 1 or 2 Modic changes.
